steps.title: Calculate the retail price
steps.content:
The retail price is 240% of the wholesale price. Convert 240% to a decimal multiplier: 240% = 2.40. Multiply the wholesale price by this factor:
$$2.40 \times 8.00 = 19.20$$
The retail price is \$19.20.

steps.title: Apply the 65% discount to find the final price
steps.content:
When something is 65% off, the customer pays the remaining percentage, not the discount amount. Remaining percentage = 100% − 65% = 35%. So multiply the retail price by 0.35:
$$0.35 \times 19.20 = 6.72$$
The discounted price is \$6.72.
